title: rhus orientalis
intro: Rhus orientalis, also known as the Chinese sumac or sumach, is a deciduous shrub native to China and the Himalayas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of gallic acid and tannins. The medicinal properties of Rhus orientalis have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for consumption, and its leaves and roots can also be used in topical applications for skin and wound care.
